text:
Quoted in Reuters today:

"The mini-glut of oil has now evaporated, with a fresh eye of a potential of disruptions ​from the Bab el-Mandeb Strait if Houthis are joining the attacks."

#oott

https://t.co/UZmYXylVi9

For context, here is what I wrote on the 6th July https://t.co/CeGXqQeXlU : 

"The current ‘mini-glut’ is a reflection on the mismatch of prompt availability of oil vs the natural trading window two months ahead for which AG barrels should now be trading September loaders instead."

What this means today is that prompt barrels are NOT coming back to the market now nor will we have a guarantee on the loading programme in August, what more in September. So it's a total flip of the situation vs a week ago.

#oott

The market is of course moving faster than my post. 

Sep ICE Murban premium vs Dubai has moved up another dollar within a few hours to +260 cpb.

It's crunch time.

#oott

Quoted in Al Jazeera today:

“Crude oil is fast losing its strategic petroleum reserve buffer and a violent repricing up cannot be discounted until the market sees toned-down rhetoric from both parties."

p/s: @Rory_Johnston is quoted too ;)

#oott

https://t.co/3czfWaL2Sc

White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett on Iran: 

"I think that there’ll be disruption [for oil], but the disruption is going to be much smaller than people expected."

President Donald Trump is “very serious” about his plan to impose a 20% toll on cargo transiting through the Strait of Hormuz, a White House official told Semafor

https://t.co/i3bbJo8Eat

U.S. GASOLINE PRICES are escalating in response to low inventories and intensifying fighting in the Gulf and Russia. Front-month futures have risen to $137 per barrel from a low of $121 a month ago. Inflation-adjusted prices are already in the 58th percentile for all months since the start of the century so far in July, up from the 14th percentile in February and a recent low of the 6th percentile in December:

3-2-1 crack spread (divide it by 3)

Gasoline 1-2

Heating oil 1-2

Brent-WTI https://t.co/h8WAW8lP49

WTI 1-2

Brent 1-2 https://t.co/iE83Fj9R6A

Fujairah, Fujairah, Fujairah. 

Crude timespreads awaken because Fujairah is where GCC has been giving stupendous discounts on crude. https://t.co/6s4NqhjQXS

Two VLCCs get attacked using the Oman lane. Origin is from Fujairah. 

Fujairah, as the ship-to-ship transit hub, is all but dead.

These tankers are sitting ducks. If Iran escalates into Fujairah, UAE will have to shut-in 3.5 million b/d of crude.

This is on par with closing Bab el-Mandeb.

Damage caused by Ukrainian drone strikes has reduced Russia's oil refining volumes to 21-year lows, commodities data and analytics firm Kpler said on Tuesday.

* Russian refinery crude runs have fallen to around 3.80 million barrels per day to date, the lowest level in well over two decades.
#oott
https://t.co/nUFaPp8Cpd
Looks like the peak in gasoline demand has been delayed once again, with the IEA having their 2027 gasoline demand estimate above the one in 2025 and 2026 #oott
Kenya extends fuel tax cut for three more months #oott  https://t.co/3ci8cW0r1j

It's going to be very challenging to effectively run a global oil industry when it's this easy to repeatedly cripple big physical assets like tankers and refineries.

Hard not to feel like this is the shape of things to come.

People are now like “but what about the oil mini-glut?! 😏”

But important to stress that there was in fact a mini-glut as Hormuz exits surged and Asian (*cough* China) import demand remained weak—it was just temporary and comparatively small, hence the “mini”.

Wouldn’t have seen prompt, contango if there wasn’t at least a brief oversupply of spot-available crude. 

Even if the drive lower was mainly paper pressure, a tighter physical market would have provided a more reliable floor before we got there.

Mini-glut now appears to be passed at lease for now, moving back to scarcity signals.

But you’re doing yourself a disservice if you only believe timespreads when they agree with your prior.

(Prompt Brent futures timespread in white, Brent Dated to Frontline—DFL—spread in blue)

Holy hell, Brent prompt timespreads are RIPPING!

From mild contango last week to >$2/bbl backwardation this morning—that’s… a heck of a lot.

Prompt backwardation spike signals that the immediate market supply-demand balance has ratcheted decisively tighter again. https://t.co/m5r6xgkXZr
Crude prices have now round-tripped the post-MOU selloff. https://t.co/rDl7zUwwLh
Gm, crude continues to run https://t.co/VbmAIGsvTc
Ukraine says 116 Russian vessels were hit in the Sea of Azov in nine days, including 11 overnight (5 tankers, 5 cargo ships, 1 tugboat). - @clashreport https://t.co/iCiiXolGOM

Tanker hit by missile - UKMTO 

6 hours ago https://t.co/PlGIPSw6uO
Russian refinery runs have declined - JP Morgan https://t.co/sl0vD9LVOx

ADNOC said the very large crude carriers Mombasa B and Al Bahyah were struck by projectiles while transiting Hormuz and had sustained "significant damage". The ministry said the vessels had been hit by Iranian cruise missiles. - Reuters
China imports of crude slowed dramatically in Q2, hitting a decade low last month at 7.1mb/d https://t.co/17Ys3YGE7W

Gold and Brent (inverted) 
Chart: Bloomberg https://t.co/4Bh8Rw8h8H
Gulf tensions and short covering drive energy higher amid renewed supply shock fears https://t.co/wKOzSNm783 via @saxobank

Interestingly, as #Brent made its latest push towards USD 85, #gold stopped falling. Instead, bullion found renewed buying interest, climbing back above USD 4,000. While the traditionally inverse relationship between oil and gold appears to be softening, it is still too early to conclude that the correlation has broken down. It may, however, signal that the market is becoming increasingly concerned about the broader economic fallout from a sustained energy shock. Tightness in refined fuel markets has already pushed diesel and gasoline prices to levels consistent with Brent trading well above USD 100 per barrel, raising the risk that growth concerns may increasingly offset inflation and rate-related headwinds for gold.

A VLCC laden with 2 million barrels of Iranian crude oil is desperately trying to reach the blockade line ahead of the 20:00 UTC deadline tonight. They usually steam at around 10kn speed.

#OOTT #IranWar #Tankers https://t.co/xBB7HuYPKf
